vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is severely compromised. P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of active foliage. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor, but the primary specimen (Pothos) is not visible or has suffered total senescence. P2 and P4 are indistinguishable in the current lighting conditions.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos absent/withered; only anchor remains)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains dark, undisturbed soil. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot appears empty or contains only substrate.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor, but the Pothos is missing or fully collapsed into the soil.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or simply shadowed; no visible cracking.
biome_observations.debris: A metal spoon is present in the T-1 image, indicating an external intervention (likely soil manipulation or feeding).

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Initial state shows minimal structure; P3 anchor is visible.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Significant reduction in ambient light; visual data becomes obscured.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Introduction of a metal spoon near P1/P2; potential soil disturbance.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Return to low-light state; no recovery of plant matter observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total failure. There is no evidence of photosynthetic activity or living plant tissue in any of the pots. The environment appears to be a 'dead' setup.
anomalies: Total absence of expected botanical specimens across all pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Across the five-day observation period, the plants have either been removed, died back completely, or are buried beneath the substrate. The presence of a spoon suggests human intervention, yet this has not resulted in the recovery of the specimens. The biome is currently non-functional.
